title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs request lines, methods, target forms, versions, and routing inputs from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the strict request-line and request-target parser. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for strict request-line and request-target parser after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.
Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Request Line Delimiters · changed case · Review Request Line Delimiters
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 1079 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
